summary: postinstall beacons installer identity to Burp Collaborator domain
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes `whoami` via child_process and reads os.hostname(), os.platform(), cwd, the CI flag, GITHUB_REPOSITORY, and NODE_ENV, then transmits them via HTTPS GET (and a DNS lookup) to a hardcoded Burp Collaborator subdomain `lg5ys3jebfzwk366pilidbmah1nsbszh.oastify.com`. The behavior is unconditional and runs on every install. While the package self-describes as a NASA VDP canary, the exfiltration is real: any installer pulling this package leaks user, host, platform, working directory, CI status, and repository identity to a third-party out-of-band server not under their control. oastify.com is Burp Suite's Collaborator service, commonly used to confirm SSRF/RCE in pentests; here it is being used as an installer-side exfiltration sink.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
